text:
Meet one of our patients.

We built her from four angles, so she looks like the same woman whether you're standing across the room from her or leaning in to check her pupils.

This is the part nobody sees when they think about medical simulation. Before the vitals move, before the case even starts, someone has to build the person.

We wanted our patients to feel real before you've done a single thing to them. The silver curls. The lines around her eyes. The blue gown that says she's already been admitted, already waiting on you.

Consistency is the hard part. Get the face slightly wrong from one angle to the next and the whole illusion breaks. So every patient in Immersa gets mapped like this first. Front, three quarter, profile, close. Locked before she ever enters a scene.

Then the physiology takes over. Her vitals move the way real biology moves. Her face stays exactly who she is.

This is the sandbox. Built one patient at a time.
